title: Weyerhaeuser Company Declares Dividend on Common Shares
author: Prnewswire
description: Weyerhaeuser Company announced a quarterly base cash dividend of $0.21 per share, payable on June 22, 2026. The company maintains a cash return framework targeting 75-80% of annual Adjusted Funds Available for Distribution (Adjusted FAD) to shareholders through base dividends, supplemental dividends, and/or share repurchases.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a consistent quarterly dividend and reaffirmed its commitment to returning 75-80% of Adjusted FAD to shareholders through a flexible capital allocation framework, demonstrating financial strength and shareholder-friendly policies.

title: Weyerhaeuser: Why the Timber Giant’s $50M-per-$10 Lumber Sensitivity Makes It a Value Trap
author: Gurufocus
description: Weyerhaeuser (WY) trades at a 39% discount to net asset value while at its deepest earnings trough in a decade. The stock offers exposure to irreplaceable timberland assets with $50M EBITDA sensitivity per $10/MBF lumber price movement. Despite Q4 2025 showing negative Wood Products EBITDA, the company maintains dividend growth and benefits from structural supply constraints in North American lumber as Canadian capacity remains impaired. The investment thesis relies on lumber price recovery from historically low levels and housing market normalization.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Trading at significant discount to NAV (0.61x) with identifiable catalysts for recovery. Company benefits from structural supply constraints, maintains dividend growth despite trough earnings, and has multiple growth drivers (NCS, TimberStrand, potential China recovery). The $50M per $10/MBF lumber sensitivity creates asymmetric upside from cyclical lows. However, the positive sentiment is tempered by current trough conditions and leverage risks.

Merged with PotlatchDeltic on January 30, 2026, creating a combined entity with 2.5x less acreage and 3x less revenue than Weyerhaeuser. Mentioned as context for WY's competitive positioning as the undisputed sector leader, but no specific investment thesis provided.

title: 2026 Seattle ORBIE Awards Recognize Top Technology Executives
author: Nicole Lammes
description: The 2026 Seattle ORBIE Awards recognized exceptional leadership from CIOs and CISOs across major Pacific Northwest organizations including Weyerhaeuser, MultiCare Health System, BECU, Starbucks, Premera Blue Cross, Remitly, PCC Community Markets, and Gesa Credit Union. The awards, hosted by SeattleCIO and PacificNorthwestCISO chapters, honored leaders driving business transformation and security excellence across nine categories. Over 450 executives attended the ceremony at The Westin Seattle.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CIO Rebecca Straka received the Global ORBIE award, recognizing exceptional leadership and technology innovation at a multi-billion dollar organization with multinational operations.
CISO Julie Ellis received the CISO Global ORBIE award, highlighting strong security leadership and business protection initiatives at a major multinational corporation.
CISO Darren Challey received the CISO Enterprise ORBIE award, recognizing effective security leadership and business protection initiatives.

title: Weyerhaeuser Company Declares Dividend on Common Shares
author: Prnewswire
description: Weyerhaeuser Company announced a quarterly base cash dividend of $0.21 per share, payable on March 20, 2026. The company maintains its cash return framework targeting 75-80% of annual Adjusted Funds Available for Distribution returned to shareholders through base dividends, supplemental dividends, and/or share repurchases.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a consistent quarterly dividend and reaffirmed its shareholder-friendly capital allocation framework targeting 75-80% of Adjusted FAD returns. This demonstrates financial stability, commitment to shareholders, and confidence in cash generation capabilities.

title: Weyerhaeuser Announces Tax Treatment of 2025 Dividend Distributions
author: Prnewswire
description: Weyerhaeuser Company announced the tax treatment of its 2025 dividend distributions totaling $0.84 per share. The dividends are designated as capital gain distributions and are not eligible for the 20% qualified business income deduction under Section 199A of the Internal Revenue Code. The company notes that tax allocations could change upon filing its 2025 tax return.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement is a routine tax disclosure for dividend distributions. While the designation as capital gains (rather than qualified dividends) may be less favorable for some shareholders, this is a standard administrative announcement with no material business impact or operational changes indicated.
